Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

Souls fall down

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2009-11-21 · 5 answers
Hare Krishna,

I have heard that when soul falls down from the spiritual world he first becomes Brahma then he falls down further in the cycle of birth and death....
Then, how can we definitely say that we have got human form of life after transmigrating through 84 lakhs of species.....

Haribol !!!
user [38] · 2009-11-21
See quotes under Satyaloka in http://www.veda.harekrsna.cz/planetarium/index.htm
user [198] · 2009-11-22
Veda Prabhu, your website says: "My first body is that of Brahma, the secondary creator of this world. I live on Satyaloka, the topmost dimensional level in this world."
But I could not find any reference for this statement. Is this statement from one of the sandarbhas or from some other scripture. What is the original source of this statement? Thank you.
user [38] · 2009-11-22
Click on >Brahma< to see the article on him with a reference to SPs purport.
user [198] · 2009-11-22
Regarding when soul falls down from the spiritual world he first becomes Brahma, Srila Prabhupada mentions in purport of Srimad Bhagavatam 9.24.58:

&amp;quot;but when the part of the Supreme Personality of Godhead unfortunately wants to enjoy independently, without Kruc0u803 su803 nu803 a, he is put into the material world, where he begins his life as Brahmu257 and is gradually degraded to the status of an ant or a worm in stool.&amp;quot;

Veda Prabhu, Do you know of any scriptural evidence supporting this statement in this purport. Thank you.
user [38] · 2009-11-22
Not yet. The same is stated by B.R. Sridhara M. in his Sri Krsna: Reality the Beautiful, ch. 4.
